sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Telecom company may have revealed iPhone 17 series spec sheet, as well as iPhone 17 and iOS 26 release dates
feed.summary: Alleged documents from South Korean carrier KT Corporation have surfaced online, potentially revealing the full spec sheet for the Apple iPhone 17 series, along with key release dates for the phones and iOS 26. The leak details specifications for the iPhone 17, 17 Air, 17 Pro, and 17 Pro Max, including screen sizes, camera setups, battery capacities, chipsets, and RAM. While the authenticity of the documents is questioned due to discrepancies and a general lack of carrier insight into full specs pre-launch, they offer a comprehensive look at what the upcoming iPhones might offer.

feed.title: Wearable Healthcare Devices Market worth US$75.98 billion by 2030 with 10.9% CAGR | MarketsandMarkets™
feed.summary: The global Wearable Healthcare Devices Market is projected to reach US$75.98 billion by 2030, growing at a CAGR of 10.9% from US$45.29 billion in 2025. This growth is fueled by increasing health awareness, a shift towards preventive care, rising chronic conditions, and technological advancements like AI-enabled analytics and real-time monitoring. Smartwatches currently hold the largest market share due to their versatile health-tracking features and integration with digital ecosystems, while North America dominates the market due to its advanced healthcare infrastructure and high adoption of digital health solutions.

feed.title: 2 Artificial Intelligence (AI) ETFs to Confidently Buy Heading Into 2026
feed.summary: This article recommends two Artificial Intelligence (AI) ETFs, the Roundhill Generative AI and Technology ETF (CHAT) and the Vanguard Mega Cap Growth ETF (MGK), for investors looking to capitalize on the AI boom heading into 2026. Both ETFs offer exposure to leading technology companies heavily involved in AI development and infrastructure. The article highlights the key holdings and performance of each ETF, suggesting them as simple ways for investors to participate in the growing AI market.

feed.title: DOMA Perpetual Sends Letter Urging Board of Directors of InMode Ltd. to Return Capital to the Shareholders
feed.summary: DOMA Perpetual Capital Management LLC, a significant stockholder of InMode Ltd. (NYSE: INMD), has sent a letter to InMode's Board of Directors, urging them to return capital to shareholders. DOMA Perpetual believes that given InMode’s substantial cash reserves (53% of its market capitalization) and strong profitability, the Board should approve and execute a 10% buyback in Q4 2025 and another 10% buyback in Q1 2026. The letter criticizes current management's leadership and negative comments regarding capital allocation, arguing that the Board has a fiduciary duty to maximize shareholder value through strategic buybacks.

feed.title: Apple introduces Apple Watch SE 3
feed.summary: Apple has introduced the next generation of Apple Watch SE, the Apple Watch SE 3, featuring advanced health capabilities like sleep score, wrist temperature sensing, and sleep apnea notifications, alongside an Always-On display, fast charging, and 5G cellular. Powered by the S10 chip, it also brings one-handed gestures, on-device Siri, and watchOS 26 innovations like Liquid Glass and Workout Buddy. Starting at $249, the Apple Watch SE 3 is available for pre-order today and in stores beginning September 19, positioned as an accessible entry point to the Apple Watch ecosystem, also offering features for kids through "Apple Watch For Your Kids."

feed.title: Webull Launches in the Netherlands to Begin EU Expansion
feed.summary: Webull Corporation has launched its retail investment platform in the Netherlands, marking the beginning of its expansion into the European Union. Its Dutch subsidiary, Webull Securities (Europe) B.V., received authorization in September 2024 and offers trading in European and U.S. equities, fractional shares, European ETFs, and U.S. options. This move aims to provide accessible and low-cost investment opportunities to European investors, with further EU countries expected to follow.

feed.title: Liberty Media CEO hints F1 nearing U.S. media deal with Apple
feed.summary: Liberty Media CEO Derek Chang indicated that the company is "pretty far along" in discussions for a U.S. media rights partner for Formula 1, strongly hinting at Apple without explicitly naming them. Chang emphasized the importance of a partner that can go beyond traditional media dealmaking to act as a sponsor, helping F1 monetize and commercialize the relationship holistically. The potential deal with Apple is seen as a way to amplify fan engagement, especially with an "F1" film set to stream on Apple TV+.

feed.title: Liberty Media CEO: ‘Pretty happy and comfortable’ with ongoing F1 media rights talks
feed.summary: The CEO of Liberty Media, Derek Chang, indicated that the company is "pretty happy and comfortable" with ongoing Formula One media rights negotiations, strongly suggesting a deal with Apple is imminent. While Apple was not named directly, Chang's comments about seeking a partner who can also act as a sponsor and enhance fan engagement align with Apple's capabilities. This move would see F1 trading ESPN's reach for potential revenue and leveraging by putting its content behind the Apple TV+ paywall, similar to Major League Soccer's strategy.

feed.title: Is Qualcomm Tesla’s Next Rival in Autonomous Driving?
feed.summary: Qualcomm (QCOM) is making significant strides in autonomous driving technology with a new partnership with BMW, challenging Tesla's dominance in the sector. Despite Qualcomm's historical undervaluation compared to its peers, this move into automotive technology, coupled with a recent analyst upgrade and favorable valuation, could position it as a strong contender in the market. The company is acting as an "arms dealer" for legacy automakers, supplying its Snapdragon Ride Pilot system to integrate Level 2+ driver-assistance features, offering investors exposure to autonomous driving without Tesla's high P/E ratio.

feed.title: Wearable Devices Ltd. Reports First Half 2025 Financial Results and Launches Mudra Link Wristband
feed.summary: Wearable Devices Ltd. reported its financial results for the first half of 2025, showing $294,000 in revenues and a reduced net loss of $3.7 million, down from $4.2 million in the previous year. The company highlighted the commercial launch of its Mudra Link wristband, expanding its market to Android and iOS users, and noted strategic collaborations, including one with a Japanese company, and advancements in military applications. CEO Asher Dahan emphasized continued investment in R&D and innovation in touchless interfaces.

feed.title: Wearable Devices Stock Surges Over 125% After Hours: Here's Why
feed.summary: Wearable Devices Ltd. (NASDAQ: WLDS) shares surged over 125% after hours following the release of its first-half 2025 financial results, which marked the commercial debut of its AI-powered Mudra Link wristband. The company reported $294,000 in revenue and significantly narrowed its net losses to $3.7 million from $4.2 million year-over-year. This optimism is further fueled by strategic moves, including a military project and expansion into the Japanese market.

feed.title: Arm launches new generation of mobile chip designs geared for AI
feed.summary: Arm Holdings has introduced its new generation of mobile chip designs, called Lumex, which are optimized for artificial intelligence to function on mobile devices without requiring internet access. These designs come in four types, catering to various devices from smart wearables to high-end smartphones, some even capable of running large AI models locally. The Lumex designs are part of Arm's strategy to provide more complete technology solutions to handset makers and chip designers, aiming to boost revenue and expand their market presence.

feed.title: Invest in Quality Stocks and Minimize Your Risk With This ETF
feed.summary: The article advocates for investing in quality stocks to minimize risk, especially in volatile markets, and highlights the JPMorgan U.S. Quality Factor ETF (JQUA) as a suitable option. This ETF selects stocks based on quality and profitability, offers good diversification with 274 holdings, and includes major companies like Alphabet, Broadcom, and Apple. JQUA has shown strong returns, rising 8% this year and nearly 90% over five years, making it a viable choice for risk-averse, long-term investors.

feed.title: B2Gold Corp stock hits 52-week high at 4.34 USD
feed.summary: B2Gold Corp's stock has reached a 52-week high of $4.34, reflecting a 58% increase over the past year and a 64% gain in the last six months. The company, with a market cap of $5.72 billion, appears undervalued according to InvestingPro analysis, which also indicates expected revenue growth of 66% this year and includes 12 additional investment insights. This milestone highlights strong investor confidence and favorable market conditions.

feed.title: Cboe Plans to Launch Cash-Settled Futures and Options on New Index Tracking Tech and Growth-Orientated U.S. Stocks
feed.summary: Cboe Global Markets announced plans to launch cash-settled futures and options on the new Cboe Magnificent 10 Index (MGTEN Index), subject to regulatory review. This new index will track 10 actively traded U.S.-listed large-cap technology and growth-oriented stocks, including the Magnificent 7, to provide targeted exposure and risk management tools for investors. The products are designed for nearly 24x5 trading, including shorter-dated options, and aim to meet the growing demand from both institutional and retail investors for derivatives on influential tech stocks.

feed.title: US stocks inch to more records as inflation slows and Oracle soars
feed.summary: US stocks, particularly the S&P 500, reached new records on Wednesday, driven by an unexpectedly positive wholesale inflation report and Oracle's robust growth forecast fueled by AI demand. Despite a slight dip in the Dow Jones Industrial Average and a flat Nasdaq, the market remains optimistic about a Federal Reserve rate cut as inflation appears to be under control. Oracle's stock surged over 35% on news of multi-billion dollar AI contracts and massive projected cloud infrastructure revenue growth.

feed.title: Wearable Devices Ltd. Skyrockets Over 500% Pre-Market: What’s Driving the Surge?
feed.summary: Wearable Devices Ltd. (NASDAQ: WLDS) saw its stock surge over 500% in pre-market trading due to the launch of its new Mudra Link wristband and positive first-half 2025 financial results, including a reduced net loss. While the company's AI-powered neural interface technology and strategic market expansions are promising, it faces challenges such as inventory write-offs, share dilution from new stock issuance, and a "going concern" warning. This article explores the company's growth catalysts and the inherent risks and rewards for traders eyeing such volatile small-cap stocks.

feed.title: Vanguard’s VUG ETF: The Ultimate Growth ETF for Your Portfolio
feed.summary: The article highlights the Vanguard Growth ETF (VUG) as a top choice for investors seeking exposure to large-cap growth stocks, particularly those benefiting from innovation and AI. VUG's strong performance, low expense ratio, and strategic holdings in tech giants like NVIDIA, Microsoft, and Apple make it an attractive option. The fund offers broad diversification compared to individual stock picking, positioning it as a core holding for long-term growth-oriented portfolios.

feed.title: Alibaba Has AI Written All Over It: Here’s Why Buying BABA Stock Makes Sense
feed.summary: Alibaba (BABA) stock has seen a significant rebound, driven by the Chinese government's support for its domestic tech companies and Alibaba's strong focus on artificial intelligence (AI). Despite mixed Q1 fiscal 2026 earnings, the company's cloud and AI businesses show promising growth, with AI-related revenue rapidly increasing. Analysts are bullish on BABA, highlighting its strategic partnerships and potential in the evolving AI landscape as key drivers for future growth.

feed.title: iShares MSCI World ETF: Rekordlauf dank Tech-Giganten
feed.summary: The iShares MSCI World ETF is experiencing a record run, largely driven by the strong performance of American technology giants like NVIDIA, Microsoft, and Apple, which constitute a significant portion (14.41%) of its portfolio. Despite this concentration, the ETF maintains broad diversification across 1,321 individual titles and boasts a 14.45% annual performance with minimal tracking error. Investors are showing growing confidence, with the fund attracting over 891 million dollars in inflows over the past year.

feed.title: Vanguard's VUG ETF: The Ultimate Growth ETF for Your Portfolio
feed.summary: The Vanguard Growth ETF (VUG) is highlighted as a premier investment for growth-oriented portfolios, driven by its focus on large-cap companies at the forefront of innovation, particularly in AI and technology. The article details its top holdings like NVIDIA, Microsoft, and Apple, its consistent outperformance against benchmarks such as the S&P 500, and its exceptionally low expense ratio. VUG is presented as a stable, diversified, and cost-effective vehicle for long-term wealth building, offering broad exposure to dynamic companies shaping the global economy.

feed.title: Corning Incorporated and Apple celebrate expanded partnership in Harrodsburg
feed.summary: Apple and Corning have expanded their partnership with a $2.5 billion investment to produce 100% of iPhone and Apple Watch cover glass at Corning's Harrodsburg facility in Kentucky. This move will bring manufacturing entirely to the U.S. and significantly boost the local economy and workforce in Harrodsburg, increasing employee levels to at least 500 positions. Apple CEO Tim Cook's presence and the commitment highlight Apple's broader investment in American manufacturing and cutting-edge technology production.

feed.title: This ETF's Growth Prospects Could Be Music to Investors' Ears
feed.summary: The MUSQ Global Music Industry Index ETF (MUSQ) is highlighted as an investment opportunity due to the music industry's significant growth projections, with Goldman Sachs forecasting a rise to $200 billion in 10 years. The article suggests MUSQ can help investors align their portfolios with personal interests while also delivering strong performance, outperforming the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 year-to-date. Key growth drivers include emerging markets and streaming subscriptions, despite initial concerns about AI disruption.

feed.title: After Earnings, Is Broadcom Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
feed.summary: Broadcom's third-quarter 2025 earnings report significantly exceeded expectations, primarily due to a new custom AI chip customer. This led Morningstar to raise its fair value estimate for Broadcom to $325 per share, up from $225, placing the stock in a "fairly valued" category despite its recent gains. The company's wide economic moat, strong financial health, and focus on software integration and operating efficiency are highlighted, though risks like customer concentration, cyclicality, and key-person risk are noted.

feed.title: Cboe Plans to Launch Contracts on New Magnificent 10 Index
feed.summary: Cboe Global Markets plans to introduce futures and options on a new Cboe Magnificent 10 Index (MGTEN Index) to cater to increased investor demand for derivatives on influential technology stocks. This new index, an equal-weighted benchmark of 10 U.S.-listed large-cap technology and growth companies, including the Magnificent 7, aims to provide targeted exposure, risk management tools, and support for short-dated and 0DTE trading strategies. The launch is anticipated in Q4 2025 for monthly options and futures, and Q1 2026 for weekly P.M.-expiring options, subject to regulatory approval.

feed.title: Target opening seven stores in October — here are the locations
feed.summary: Target is set to open seven new stores in October 2025, with six of these locations exceeding the company's average store size of 125,000 sq. ft., signaling a continued focus on larger footprints. These openings are part of Target's plan to open 20 new stores this year and over 300 new stores in the next decade. The new stores are located across Arizona, Florida, Nebraska, South Carolina, Virginia, California, and Texas, and will feature in-store partners like Apple, CVS Pharmacy, Disney store, Starbucks, and Target Optical.

feed.title: Tim Cook explains why Apple is investing heavily in Corning's glass factory
feed.summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook detailed why the company is investing $2.5 billion in Corning's Kentucky glass factory, highlighting the glass manufacturer's innovation, cost-effectiveness, and quality. The investment will boost domestic manufacturing, create jobs, and triple production of specialized glass for iPhones and Apple Watches, utilizing Corning's strong ceramic shield technology. This partnership builds on a long-standing relationship between Apple and Corning, with the factory becoming a world-leading site for highly specialized glass production.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Calls Tandem Diabetes “Speculative Stock for Younger People”
feed.summary: Jim Cramer commented on Tandem Diabetes Care, Inc. (NASDAQ: TNDM), calling it a "speculative stock for younger people." He noted that while the company keeps losing money, the personal use of its products by young investors might make it an encouraged, albeit risky, choice. The article also highlights that Insider Monkey believes some AI stocks offer greater promise for higher returns in a shorter timeframe than TNDM.

feed.title: iShares MSCI World ETF: KI-Boom treibt Rekordjagd
feed.summary: The iShares MSCI World ETF is experiencing a record hunt driven by the AI boom, primarily due to its strong focus on US technology stocks like NVIDIA and Microsoft. While this concentration has led to outstanding performance, it also carries significant risks due to high dependency on a few mega-cap companies. The article questions the sustainability of this strategy given valuation concerns and the influence of interest rate expectations on investor risk appetite.

feed.title: Alphabet enters $3 trillion market cap club as Big Tech's AI momentum builds
authors: Johann M Cherian and Shashwat Chauhan
feed.summary: Google parent Alphabet achieved a $3 trillion market capitalization for the first time, driven by renewed optimism in artificial intelligence and a favorable antitrust ruling. Its shares have rallied over 32% this year, making it the best performer among the "Magnificent 7" stocks. This milestone positions Alphabet alongside Apple and Microsoft in the $3 trillion club, with a recent court ruling allowing it to retain control of its Chrome browser and Android operating system.

feed.title: Chenghe Acquisition III prices $110 million IPO on NASDAQ By Investing.com
feed.summary: Chenghe Acquisition III Co. (CHECU) has priced its initial public offering at $110 million, offering 11 million units at $10.00 each. The special purpose acquisition company (SPAC) plans to list its units on the Nasdaq Global Market on September 16, 2025, with Class A ordinary shares and warrants later trading separately under "CHEC" and "CHECW." The company, incorporated in the Cayman Islands, intends to acquire growing companies in Asian markets or global firms with an Asian presence.

feed.title: This Central KY factory will make glass for every iPhone and Apple Watch
feed.summary: Apple is investing $2.5 billion to move all iPhone and Apple Watch glass production to a Corning Inc. facility in Harrodsburg, Kentucky. This expansion will create at least 200 new hourly jobs and some salaried positions. The partnership also includes the establishment of an Apple-Corning Innovation Center for advanced materials research, underscoring Apple's commitment to American manufacturing.

feed.title: Direxion's QQQU/QQQD ETFs Facilitate Speculation For Magnificent Seven Stocks
feed.summary: Direxion offers two ETFs, QQQU and QQQD, for speculating on the "Magnificent Seven" stocks. The QQQU ETF is a leveraged bull fund tracking 200% of the Indxx Magnificent 7 Index, while the QQQD ETF provides 100% inverse performance for bearish investors. These ETFs allow traders to gain exposure to the Mag 7's performance without the complexities of options, though they carry unique risks such as higher volatility and suitability for short-term holding due to daily compounding effects.

feed.title: Looking to buy an industrial AI play? Here are levels to watch for Eaton and GE Vernova
feed.summary: This article provides technical analysis and potential entry points for Eaton (ETN) and GE Vernova (GEV), two industrial AI stocks benefiting from the data center boom. It emphasizes the importance of both fundamental and technical analysis for investment decisions, outlining specific price levels to watch for both stocks, particularly focusing on moving averages and support/resistance levels. The author suggests conservative and aggressive approaches for investors looking to establish or increase positions in these companies.

feed.title: IWY ETF Price at $270.12: Outperformance Driven by Mega-Cap Tech
feed.summary: The iShares Russell Top 200 Growth ETF (IWY) has climbed to $270.12, showcasing significant outperformance with a 25.34% yearly gain driven by its concentrated holdings in mega-cap tech giants like NVIDIA and Microsoft. The ETF is positioned to benefit from the AI capital expenditure supercycle and is projected to reach the $300-$310 range within the next year, despite its premium valuation. Its strategic weighting methodology balances concentration risk while leveraging strong growth prospects from its top holdings.

feed.title: 4 Must-Own Dividend-Growth Stocks
feed.summary: This article highlights four dividend-growth stocks—Microsoft, Broadcom, Apple, and Visa—that are among the top holdings of two highly-rated dividend-growth funds, Vanguard Dividend Growth and T. Rowe Price Dividend Growth. These companies are characterized by strong financial health, wide economic moats, and consistent dividend increases, making them attractive for long-term investors when purchased at fair value or below.

feed.title: QQQ ETF: Hơn cả một chiếc rổ cổ phiếu công nghệ
feed.summary: QQQ ETF (Invesco QQQ ETF) mô phỏng hiệu suất của chỉ số NASDAQ-100, bao gồm 100 công ty phi tài chính lớn nhất trên Nasdaq, chủ yếu là các công ty công nghệ hàng đầu như Apple, Microsoft, Amazon và Nvidia. Quỹ này thu hút nhà đầu tư toàn cầu nhờ khả năng tiếp cận nhanh chóng với các cổ phiếu tăng trưởng mạnh trong ngành công nghệ, phù hợp với những nhà đầu tư có khẩu vị rủi ro trung bình đến cao và tầm nhìn dài hạn, mặc dù đi kèm với rủi ro tập trung cao và biến động mạnh. Bài viết cũng so sánh QQQ với các biến thể QQQM và QQQJ, cung cấp cái nhìn chi tiết về cấu trúc danh mục, hiệu suất, chi phí và rủi ro liên quan, giúp nhà đầu tư đưa ra quyết định phù hợp với mục tiêu tài chính cá nhân.

feed.title: Amkor pops as Apple CEO Cook says company will help with packaging (AMKR:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: Amkor Technology shares surged nearly 8% in premarket trading after Apple CEO Tim Cook announced the semiconductor packaging company would assist Apple with its packaging and reshoring initiatives. This collaboration highlights Apple's efforts to diversify its supply chain and bring more manufacturing processes closer to home. The news is expected to have a positive impact on Amkor Technology's business outlook.

feed.title: SPYI: A Good Case For New Type Of Dividend Growth (BATS:SPYI)
feed.summary: SPYI is presented as a strong long-term buy for income-focused investors, offering an 11% yield with monthly distributions and consistent NAV growth. Its S&P 500-backed portfolio provides stability, supporting sustainable and potentially growing distributions. The article suggests SPYI offers more reliable income than individual dividend stocks, making it suitable for retirement withdrawal strategies.

feed.title: Under Armour’s CEO says micromanagement is ‘totally underestimated’ in business: ‘There’s too much lost on pretense’
feed.summary: Under Armour CEO Kevin Plank believes micromanagement is "totally underestimated" and embraces it at certain levels, focusing on quickly finding correct solutions rather than being bogged down by pretense or process. He operates on an 80-20 rule, where most of the business is structured for speed to market, allowing a smaller portion for flexible thinking. Plank also noted that despite its bad reputation, studies indicate micromanagement can improve short-term productivity and skill upgrading, although it carries risks like reduced autonomy and burnout.

feed.title: ULTY ETF is a 117% yielding fund, beating VOO: is it a buy?
feed.summary: The YieldMax Ultra Option Income Strategy ETF (ULTY) has performed well this year, reaching a record high and offering a reported 117% dividend yield. Unlike typical covered call ETFs, ULTY's performance, driven by its manager's stock selection based on implied volatility, has outpaced the S&P 500's benchmark ETF (VOO) in total returns. However, the fund carries risks such as potential NAV erosion, capped upside due to options strategies, and reliance on the manager's expertise.

feed.title: Alphabet has reached a market capitalization of $3 trillion for the first time. What does the company earn the most from?
feed.summary: Alphabet Inc. has achieved a $3 trillion market capitalization, making it the third American corporation to do so. This growth is primarily attributed to advancements in artificial intelligence and a favorable US court ruling concerning its Chrome browser and Android operating system. The company's advertising business remains a major earner, alongside increasing profits from its cloud services, driven by investments in chips and the Gemini AI model.

feed.title: Why Amkor (AMKR) Stock Is Trading Up Today
feed.summary: Shares of Amkor Technology (AMKR) rose by 4.6% after Apple CEO Tim Cook highlighted the company as a key partner in Apple's U.S. manufacturing strategy, reinforcing its role in the "reshoring" initiative. This positive news overshadowed recent concerns about Amkor's inventory due to a production halt for Nvidia's H20 AI chip, indicating that the market views the Apple partnership as a significant positive factor. Despite the H20 chip issue, the stock's year-to-date performance is positive, with a 2.3% gain, although it remains below its 52-week high.

feed.title: Digital Student IDs Come to Apple & Google Wallets: Transact IDX Transforms Campus Access for Millions
feed.summary: Transact IDX, a campus transaction and credential system by Transact + CBORD, has released a major update allowing students to add their campus ID directly to Apple and Google Wallets. This integration aims to improve security, reduce IT support, and enhance student adoption by making campus access more convenient. The update also includes other enhancements like consolidated student access to balances and credentials, expanded partner integrations, and automated administrative tasks for staff.

feed.title: iShares MSCI World ETF: Konzentration als Fluch oder Segen?
feed.summary: The iShares MSCI World ETF (URTH) is characterized by a high concentration in US companies (over 70%) and a strong focus on the tech sector, with the top ten holdings accounting for almost 25% of the fund's assets. While this concentration has led to impressive returns, it also raises questions about diversification and vulnerability to market fluctuations. An analysis from February 20 suggests urgent action may be needed for investors in this ETF.

feed.title: Energy Vault (NRGV) CEO Piconi buys $115k in shares By Investing.com
feed.summary: Energy Vault Holdings, Inc. CEO Robert Piconi recently purchased 50,000 shares of the company's common stock for approximately $115,120. This transaction follows a significant stock price increase of over 98% in the past six months, despite the company facing profitability challenges and missing Q2 2025 earnings and revenue forecasts. Energy Vault has also regained compliance with the NYSE minimum listing requirements.

feed.title: ICICI Bank to provide discount/cashback of up to ₹50,000 as part of its ‘Festive Bonanza’
feed.summary: ICICI Bank has launched its ‘Festive Bonanza’ offering discounts or cashback of up to ₹50,000 across various categories like e-commerce, smartphones, electronics, fashion, and travel. Customers can avail these offers using credit/debit cards, internet banking, cardless EMI, or consumer finance, with no-cost EMI options available. The bank has partnered with major brands including Apple, Flipkart, Croma, OnePlus, MakeMyTrip, and also introduced special offers on various loan products such as home loans, auto loans, and personal loans.

feed.title: Nvidia invests $5bn in Intel
feed.summary: Nvidia Corp. has agreed to invest $5 billion in Intel Corp., a surprising move given their historical rivalry, to co-develop chips for PCs and data centers. This investment will involve Nvidia buying Intel common stock and the companies integrating Nvidia's graphics technology into Intel's PC chips and Intel's processors into Nvidia's data center products. The collaboration aims to strengthen Intel’s market position against competitors like AMD and leverage Nvidia’s dominance in AI computing.

feed.title: Tiny "Titan" Timing Chip: How SiTime’s Pinhead-Sized Clock Could Revolutionize Wearable Tech
feed.summary: SiTime (NASDAQ:SITM) has unveiled its new "Titan" timing chip, a pinhead-sized MEMS resonator designed to revolutionize wearable technology. This tiny component, measuring 0.46 × 0.46 mm, is 4x smaller, 50% more power-efficient, and 50x more shock-resistant than traditional quartz crystals, making it ideal for space-constrained, battery-powered gadgets like smartwatches, earbuds, and medical implants. The Titan chip is poised to capture a significant share of the $4 billion resonator market by enabling smaller, smarter, and longer-lasting wearable devices, and industry experts view it as a game-changer for the timing industry.

feed.title: Prudential and LPL Collaborate to Expand Access to Retirement Security
feed.summary: Prudential Financial, Inc. and LPL Financial LLC are expanding their collaboration to offer an Insurance Overlay retirement lifetime income strategy for LPL’s managed accounts platform. This initiative aims to provide LPL's 29,000 financial advisors with Prudential’s insurance-based retirement products, addressing longevity and market volatility risks for clients. The collaboration builds on a recent strategic integration of Prudential Advisors onto the LPL Financial wealth management platform, seeking to expand access to retirement security amidst a growing aging population.

feed.title: Starbucks Ruined by CEO Niccol
feed.summary: Starbucks CEO Brian Niccol's first year has seen the company's stock underperform significantly compared to the S&P 500, with revenue growth off track and comparable store sales declining. Despite various operational changes and a plan for China expansion, Niccol's efforts have not yet resulted in a turnaround for Starbucks, facing challenges like shaky consumer spending and increased competition in key markets. The article questions whether these strategies will improve financial metrics in upcoming quarterly results.

feed.title: Apple’s iPhone 17 series launches in Taiwan
feed.summary: Apple's iPhone 17 series has launched in Taiwan, with major telecoms offering incentives and enthusiasts queuing days in advance. The new series includes four models, priced from NT$29,900, with double the minimum storage at 256GB. A dedicated customer, Mr. Hsu, was the first in line for the third consecutive year at Apple's direct store, highlighting his interest in the upgraded cameras.

feed.title: ICICI Bank’s ‘Festive Bonanza’ Returns With Blockbuster Deals Across Top Brands.
feed.summary: ICICI Bank has relaunched its annual 'Festive Bonanza', offering substantial discounts and cashback of up to ₹50,000 across various categories like online shopping, mobiles, electronics, fashion, and travel. Customers can access these deals using their credit/debit cards, internet banking, cardless EMI, and consumer finance, with no-cost EMI options also available. The bank has partnered with major brands including Apple, Flipkart, Croma, and MakeMyTrip, and is also offering special deals on loan products such as home, auto, personal, and loan against securities.

feed.title: Wearable Devices (NASDAQ: WLDS) Spotlighted in NetworkNewsWire Editorial on AI-Powered Wearables
feed.summary: Wearable Devices (NASDAQ: WLDS) was featured in a NetworkNewsWire (NNW) editorial titled "Gesture-Control Wearables Redefine Human-Technology Interaction," highlighting the increasing demand for AI-enabled, touch-free wearables. The article positions Wearable Devices as a leader in neural input solutions, showcasing its Mudra Band and Mudra Link products. These innovations enable intuitive, touch-free control of digital devices using gestures, improving human-technology interaction across various sectors.

feed.title: iShares ESG Aware MSCI USA Growth ETF(EGUS) - 常見問題與關鍵問答-股市爆料同學會
feed.summary: This article provides key information and a Q&A section for the iShares ESG Aware MSCI USA Growth ETF (EGUS). It details the ETF's current price, performance metrics across various timeframes, top five holdings including Nvidia, Apple, and Microsoft, and explains the significance of real-time price trends for investment decisions. The page also invites user discussion but currently shows no recent posts.

feed.title: Oklo, Apple, FedEx, Lennar, AGM Group: Stocks Making The Biggest Moves Today
feed.summary: This article highlights several stocks that made significant moves on a Friday, driven by various corporate and political developments. Oklo Inc. saw a surge due to a technology deal, Apple Inc. gained from strong iPhone 17 sales, and FedEx Corp. beat Wall Street expectations with its Q1 results. Conversely, Lennar Corp. shares fell after reporting a drop in Q3 profit, while AGM Group Holdings Inc. skyrocketed following the sale of a subsidiary.

feed.title: iPhone 17 Pro in new colors top choice among Apple fans: Chunghwa Telecom
feed.summary: Chunghwa Telecom reports strong demand for Apple's new iPhone 17 series in Taiwan, with the iPhone 17 Pro being the top choice among its clients. The new Cosmic Orange and Deep Blue colors for the iPhone 17 Pro, and Sky Blue for the iPhone Air, are particularly popular. Other distributors like Far EasTone and 3C retailers also experienced high sales, with some models selling out within minutes, indicating robust replacement demand in the market.

feed.title: 3 reasons why the iShares S&P 500 ETF (IVV) is still a long-term buy
feed.summary: The iShares S&P 500 ETF (IVV) is highlighted as a strong long-term investment due to its impressive average annual return of 15.3% over the last decade. Key reasons include potential tailwinds from falling US interest rates, its portfolio of high-performing businesses like Nvidia and Apple, and exceptionally low management fees of 0.04%, which enhances compounding growth for investors.

feed.title: Affirm Holdings grants equity awards to executive officers
feed.summary: Affirm Holdings announced that its Compensation Committee approved annual equity awards for certain executive officers, including Rob O’Hare, Katherine Adkins, Michael Linford, and Libor Michalek. These awards, granted on September 18, comprise both restricted stock units (RSUs) and performance stock units (PSUs) that vest into shares of Affirm’s Class A common stock. The PSUs are tied to time-based, service-based, and company financial performance conditions over a three-year period beginning July 1, 2025.

feed.title: Is Schwab Fundamental U.S. Large Company ETF (FNDX) a Strong ETF Right Now?
feed.summary: The article evaluates the Schwab Fundamental U.S. Large Company ETF (FNDX), highlighting its smart beta strategy focusing on large-cap value stocks. It details the fund's sponsor, index, expense ratio, sector exposure, top holdings, and performance metrics. The piece also suggests alternative ETFs for investors to consider within the same market segment.

feed.title: Apple launches new project to protect and restore California redwood forest
feed.summary: Apple has announced a new investment in collaboration with The Conservation Fund to protect and sustainably manage 14,000 acres of coastal redwood forest in California. This project is part of Apple's expanded Restore Fund initiative, which now includes two dozen conservation and regenerative agriculture projects across six continents, aiming to achieve carbon neutrality by 2030. The initiative focuses on nature-based carbon removal solutions to balance remaining emissions after reducing global emissions by 75 percent.

feed.title: Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOO) Top Holdings
feed.summary: This article details the top holdings of the Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOO), highlighting its market-cap weighted structure and heavy reliance on mega-cap technology companies like NVIDIA, Microsoft, and Apple. It explains how this concentration drives returns while also pointing out the inherent risks and how VOO serves as a low-cost, broad-market investment vehicle. The article also provides a deep dive into the top three holdings, explaining their business models and contributions to VOO's performance.

feed.title: SPDR S&P 500 ETF (SPLG) Top 25 Holdings
feed.summary: This article details the top 25 holdings of the SPDR S&P 500 ETF (SPLG), a low-cost option for U.S. large-cap market exposure. It highlights the significant concentration of the fund in a few mega-cap tech giants like NVIDIA, Microsoft, and Apple, which collectively account for over 21% of the ETF. The piece explains the rationale behind this concentration, its implications for performance, and the individual strengths of the top three holdings.

feed.title: Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) Top 25 Holdings
feed.summary: This article details the top 25 holdings of the Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ), an ETF that tracks the Nasdaq-100. It highlights QQQ's concentration in growth-oriented tech giants, particularly NVIDIA, Microsoft, and Apple, which collectively account for a significant portion of the fund's weight. The piece explains how these key holdings drive QQQ's performance and innovation, while also noting the fund's sensitivity to the tech cycle and its role as a growth engine rather than a total-market proxy.

feed.title: Disney Raises Streaming Prices: Is Disney+ Now More Expensive Than Netflix?
feed.summary: Disney has announced its third price increase in three years for Disney+ and Hulu, effective October 21, 2025, making some of its plans more expensive than competitors like Netflix. The ad-supported Disney+ plan will now be $11.99 and the ad-free premium plan will be $18.99, positions Disney+ to have the most expensive ad-supported and ad-free plans among listed services. This move comes as consumers are already feeling the pinch of rising streaming costs, with surveys indicating many believe they pay too much and would cancel subscriptions if prices increase further.

feed.title: Why Is Netskope, Inc. Class A Common Stock (NTSK) Stock Down Today?
feed.summary: Netskope, Inc. Class A Common Stock (NTSK) is down 2.01% today, trading at $10.25 with elevated volume. The stock is currently below both its 50-day and 200-day moving averages, indicating short-term and long-term weakness. Despite a bullish analyst sentiment, Meyka's AI assigns NTSK a moderate B grade, and forecast models project negative returns over one month, one year, and five years.

feed.title: General Motors Senior VP Alan Wexler to retire
feed.summary: General Motors announced that Senior VP Alan Wexler will retire on October 31st after five years with the company, having previously served as Chairman and CEO of Publicis Sapient. His contributions included shaping strategic priorities, enhancing data usage, and strengthening tech partnerships. Concurrently, Zach Kirkman will transition to the newly created role of Vice President, Strategy and Corporate Development, reporting to CEO Mary Barra, bringing experience from Apple and Tesla.

feed.title: China's Nasdaq 100 Equivalent: Rayliant and ChinaAMC Launch Transformative Tech ETF Tracking 100 Top Firms
feed.summary: Rayliant Investment Research and China Asset Management Co., Ltd. (ChinaAMC) have launched the Rayliant-ChinaAMC Transformative China Tech ETF (NASDAQ: CNQQ). This new ETF aims to provide investors with exposure to approximately 100 top Chinese and Hong Kong-listed technology companies, acting as "China's Nasdaq 100." The fund uses a factor-based, forward-looking approach to target companies in innovative and transformative industries, such as automotive, health tech, and digital technology, across both mainland China and Hong Kong markets.

feed.title: Warren Buffett Stocks: Berkshire Hathaway Portfolio September 2025
feed.summary: This article details Berkshire Hathaway's stock portfolio as of September 2025, highlighting recent buys, sells, and current holdings. It provides insight into Warren Buffett's investment strategy, including his value investing philosophy and emphasis on long-term holds, while cautioning against direct copy-trading.

feed.title: The Club Is Yours! Play Your Way in EA SPORTS FC™ 26, Now Available Worldwide
feed.summary: Electronic Arts has launched EA SPORTS FC™ 26 and the EA SPORTS FC™ Mobile 26 Update worldwide, inviting players to experience community-driven football across various platforms. The new releases feature overhauled gameplay based on community feedback, new archetypes, and expanded tactical flexibility, alongside unparalleled authenticity with licenses for major leagues and competitions. Players are also encouraged to participate in the FC Feedback Hub to help shape the future of the game.

feed.title: Morning Wrap: ASX futures point up, S&P 500 posts losing streak, gold advances, Emeco in focus
feed.summary: ASX 200 futures are up slightly, while the S&P 500 experienced its longest losing streak in a month. Gold and gold miners saw advances overnight, potentially benefiting ASX-listed gold stocks. Mining services group Emeco Holdings is in focus due to reports of a potential takeover bid.

feed.title: The Club Is Yours! Play Your Way in EA SPORTS FC™ 26, Now Available Worldwide
feed.summary: Electronic Arts Inc. announced the worldwide launch of EA SPORTS FC™ 26 and the EA SPORTS FC™ Mobile 26 Update, offering community-driven football experiences across multiple platforms. The new titles introduce overhauled gameplay based on player feedback, including distinct gameplay presets and new features for Career and Football Ultimate Team modes, alongside significant mobile game improvements and exclusive in-game MLS content. Players are encouraged to provide feedback through the FC Feedback Hub to help shape the future of the game.

feed.title: S&P500 and Nasdaq 100: US Stocks Rise Today After PCE Report; Intel, Paccar Jump
feed.summary: U.S. stock futures rose after the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) inflation report matched forecasts, calming fears of tighter Federal Reserve policy. Despite recent losses, the S&P 500, Nasdaq 100, and Dow futures saw gains, with Intel surging on investment news and Paccar jumping due to new tariff threats. Traders are now focusing on upcoming Fed speeches and economic data to inform future rate cut expectations.

feed.title: Prediction: These 3 Growth ETFs Could Crush the S&P 500 Over the Long Term
feed.summary: This article identifies three growth ETFs—Schwab U.S. Large-Cap Growth ETF (SCHG), iShares Core S&P 500 Growth ETF (IVW), and Vanguard Information Technology ETF (VGT)—that are predicted to outperform the S&P 500 over the long term. It highlights their historical performance, specific holdings, and outlines the risk-reward profiles for investors considering these funds. The Vanguard Information Technology ETF, in particular, is noted for its explosive returns but also higher volatility due to its tech-heavy focus.

feed.title: Prediction: 1 Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock Will Be Worth More Than Nvidia and Palantir Combined by 2030
feed.summary: Alphabet, despite its current valuation being lower than the combined market caps of Nvidia and Palantir, is predicted to surpass them by 2030 due to its strong growth opportunities in AI-enhanced search, cloud computing, and autonomous driving via Waymo. While Nvidia faces risks from custom AI chips and Palantir's valuation is considered extremely high, Alphabet's diversified AI integration and relatively cheaper stock price position it for significant future growth. The article argues that Alphabet's vertically integrated cloud business and its consumer-facing AI advancements make it a strong contender for becoming the world's largest company.

feed.title: Bluerock Homes Trust Completes Acquisition of Skytop Apartments
feed.summary: Bluerock Homes Trust acquired Skytop Apartments, a 361-unit residential community in Cincinnati, Ohio, for $88.5 million. The acquisition was financed through company equity, a Fannie Mae senior loan, and borrowings from an existing credit facility with KeyBank. The most recent analyst rating on BHM stock is a Buy with a $15.00 price target.

feed.title: Best-Performing Leveraged ETFs of Last Week
feed.summary: Last week, despite a downbeat Wall Street performance with the S&P 500, Dow, and Nasdaq all declining, several leveraged ETFs posted strong gains. These top performers included leveraged ETFs tracking Intel, Webull, Lucid, and Marvell Technology, driven by company-specific positive news such as Intel's investments, Webull's European expansion, Lucid's increased production targets, and Marvell Technology's optimistic outlook. The broader market context included stable inflation data, slightly weaker consumer sentiment, robust Q2 GDP growth, and a Fed rate cut to address a weakening labor market, alongside renewed tariff tensions.

feed.title: Avoid JEPQ and buy the 14% yielding QQQI ETF instead
feed.summary: The article advocates for the NEOS Nasdaq-100 (R) High Income ETF (QQQI) over the JPMorgan Nasdaq Equity Premium Income ETF (JEPQ), citing QQQI's higher dividend yield of 14% compared to JEPQ's 10% and its superior year-to-date and 12-month total returns. It highlights QQQI's advanced covered call strategies, which include tax minimization through "Section 1256" contracts and tax loss harvesting, making it a more attractive option for income investors despite a slightly higher expense ratio.

feed.title: Why JEPQ’s ~10% Yield Comes with Hidden Trade-Offs
feed.summary: The JPMorgan Nasdaq Equity Premium Income ETF (JEPQ) offers a high annual yield, monthly payouts, and exposure to major technology stocks. However, this comes with trade-offs including higher fees, less diversification compared to index funds like SPY and VOO, and lower share-price performance. Investors should weigh these benefits and disadvantages carefully before investing.

feed.title: Baron Technology Fund Q2 2025 Shareholder Letter
feed.summary: The Baron Technology Fund significantly outperformed its benchmark, the MSCI ACWI Information Technology Index, during the second quarter of 2025, driven by strong gains from companies like Broadcom, NVIDIA, and Spotify. The fund emphasizes active management and investment in technology winners with sustainable competitive advantages, particularly those benefiting from transformative trends like AI and cloud computing. The letter discusses the impact of AI on the market, recent M&A activity, and the fund's strategic portfolio adjustments, including increased positions in semiconductor companies and sales due to regulatory concerns.

feed.title: Motorola Solutions, Inc. Experiences Valuation Adjustment Amidst Competitive Market Landscape
feed.summary: Motorola Solutions, Inc. has adjusted its valuation to $456.52, exhibiting a P/E ratio of 34 and an impressive 126.04% return on equity. While its one-year stock return of 1.88% lagged behind the S&P 500, the company has delivered a significant 196.44% return over the past five years, outperforming the S&P 500. These financial metrics highlight the company's strong standing in a competitive market.

feed.title: Simon Begins Redevelopment of Shops at Chestnut Hill in Metro Boston
feed.summary: Simon Property Group has initiated a redevelopment project for The Shops at Chestnut Hill, a major shopping, dining, and entertainment complex located near Boston. The renovation will encompass repainting interiors, installing new lighting, updating artwork, refreshing the indoor garden, and upgrading flooring, facades, and restrooms. The project is expected to be largely completed by the end of the year, enhancing a center that hosts approximately 50 retail and restaurant establishments, including the only Bloomingdale's in Massachusetts.

feed.title: JEPQ ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns EURONEXT:JEPQ
feed.summary: This article provides an analysis of the JEPQ ETF (JPMORGAN ETFS (IRELAND) ICAV - NASDAQ Equity Premium Income Active Ucits ETF USD), focusing on its dividends and returns. It details key statistics such as Assets Under Management (AUM), dividend yield, expense ratio, and holdings, primarily in Electronic Technology and Technology Services sectors. The ETF, issued by JPMorgan Chase & Co., has an inception date of October 29, 2024, and pays monthly dividends.

feed.title: Logitech Adds Vibration Feedback to Popular MX Mouse Line
feed.summary: Logitech International SA has announced the MX Master 4 mouse, featuring haptic feedback. This new version aims to enhance efficiency for creative professionals by integrating video game-like vibrations for interacting with software and frequently used tools in applications like Adobe Photoshop. The $120 mouse will be available in October for both Windows and Mac users.

feed.title: How to Use TradingView | A Beginner’s Guide
feed.summary: This beginner's guide introduces TradingView, a highly popular platform for trade analysis used by over 50 million traders. It covers the platform's features, including various charting tools, indicators, and how to use them for different asset classes like stocks, crypto, and forex. The article also highlights how eToro clients can access TradingView's functionalities.

feed.title: The Roundup: Top Takeaways from Oaktree’s Quarterly Letters – September 2025 Edition
feed.summary: Oaktree's September 2025 "Roundup" highlights current market bifurcations, with Howard Marks cautioning about worrisome equity valuations driven by the S&P 493's high P/E ratios rather than just the "Magnificent Seven." The report also discusses value opportunities in credit markets due to diverging valuations, potential disruptions in the highly leveraged healthcare sector, and the emerging need for structured equity solutions in private equity due to low DPI. Finally, it notes the re-establishment of momentum in real estate debt, especially with record CMBS issuance, signaling renewed investor confidence despite past challenges.

feed.title: Opera launches agentic AI browser Neon for power users
feed.summary: Opera Limited has launched Opera Neon, an AI-powered browser designed for power users, featuring "Tasks" for organizing complex web activities and "Cards" for reusable AI prompt instructions. This subscription-based browser aims to execute tasks on behalf of users, leveraging local processing for security. Opera, with a market cap of $1.83 billion, shows strong business momentum and its stock is considered undervalued by InvestingPro.

feed.title: U.S. Commerce head Lutnick wants Taiwan to help America make 50% of its chips locally
feed.summary: U.S. Secretary of Commerce Howard Lutnick has proposed a "50-50" split with Taiwan in semiconductor production for the U.S. market, aiming for America to produce half of its chips domestically. This move seeks to reduce American reliance on Taiwan, which currently produces over 90% of the world's advanced semiconductors. Lutnick believes that increased domestic production would enhance U.S. security and argues that a more balanced chip production between the two nations would also make Taiwan safer.

feed.title: TTXU Stock Fund Price and Chart — AMEX:TTXU
feed.summary: This article provides detailed information on the TTXU Direxion Daily Technology Top 5 Bull 2X ETF, including its current price, performance metrics, and key financial statistics like AUM, expense ratio, and dividend yield. It describes TTXU as a 2x leveraged ETF focused on the top 5 US information technology companies, emphasizing its design as a short-term trading vehicle due to its leveraged nature and daily resetting factor. The article also covers the fund's structure, management style, and answers frequently asked questions about ETFs and TTXU specifically.

feed.title: Texas Instruments to lay off 160-plus at fabs
feed.summary: Texas Instruments plans to lay off over 160 employees at its fabrication plants in North Dallas and Sherman as it phases out older silicon wafer production. This move is part of the company's strategy to invest tens of billions in new factories while discontinuing an older method of semiconductor manufacturing. The layoffs are linked to the company's broader transition away from obsolete wafer technology.

feed.title: The Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLK) Top 25 Holdings
feed.summary: The Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLK) provides investors with exposure to U.S. large-cap technology companies, tracking the S&P 500's technology sector. The fund is highly concentrated, with NVIDIA, Microsoft, and Apple comprising nearly 40% of its holdings, reflecting the influence of AI, cloud infrastructure, and digital ecosystems. XLK has demonstrated strong performance in 2025, driven by these tech giants and their continued innovation.

feed.title: EPAM and BOSS Unveil a Revolutionary Immersive Spatial Experience, Powered by Apple Vision Pro, That Redefines Motorsport Fandom
feed.summary: EPAM Systems, Inc. announced a collaboration with BOSS to launch a revolutionary immersive spatial experience powered by Apple Vision Pro, redefining motorsport fandom. This innovation transports fans into the high-octane world of racing, creating unforgettable moments at the intersection of fashion, retail, sport, and technology. The experience debuts on October 2, 2025, at select BOSS locations in seven cities, coinciding with the Formula 1® Singapore Airlines Singapore Grand Prix 2025.

feed.title: Warren Buffett anuncia maior aquisição em 3 anos e compra OxyChem por US$ 9,7 bi
feed.summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway has agreed to acquire Occidental Petroleum Corp.'s petrochemical division, OxyChem, for approximately US$9.7 billion in cash. This marks Berkshire's largest acquisition in three years, signaling Buffett's return to major transactions after a period of reducing significant positions. The deal allows Occidental to significantly reduce its debt, although some analysts expect it to impact future cash flow and trading multiples due to OxyChem's role as a growth driver and source of diversification.

feed.title: Mass layoffs hit Texas Fortune 500 company
feed.summary: Texas Instruments is laying off approximately 183 employees as it shuts down a 150mm production line at its DFAB worksite in Dallas. This comes despite a recent announcement that Texas Instruments would be a recipient of Apple's $600 billion investment in U.S. manufacturing to produce chips for iPhones and iPads. The layoffs confirm widespread rumors and highlight ongoing concerns about job security at the company, which has a history of workforce fluctuations.

feed.title: UDR Appoints Richard B. Clark to Board
feed.summary: UDR, Inc. has appointed Richard B. Clark to its Board of Directors as part of a long-term succession plan, expanding the board to nine members. Clark is a seasoned real estate executive with extensive experience in capital markets and property management. He will serve on the Nominating and Governance Committee and the Audit and Risk Management Committee, and currently, analyst sentiment for UDR stock is a Hold with a $40.00 price target.

feed.title: TDV vs. TDIV: Talking Tech Dividends With ETFs
feed.summary: This article compares two technology dividend ETFs, TDIV and TDV, which offer investors different approaches to gaining exposure to dividend-paying tech companies. TDV focuses on "Dividend Aristocrats" with at least seven consecutive years of payout increases and uses an equal-weighting methodology, while TDIV has a broader selection of tech and communication services stocks with a minimum yield and a dividend value-weighted approach. The piece highlights their differing methodologies, holdings, performance, and suitability for various investor profiles.

feed.title: Should I offload Fundsmith Equity and buy a global or S&P 500 index fund instead?
feed.summary: The author, an investor in Fundsmith Equity, is questioning whether to switch to global or S&P 500 index funds due to Fundsmith's recent underperformance compared to these benchmarks. Despite the appeal of greater tech exposure and diversified stock selection offered by an S&P 500 ETF, the author expresses concern about the S&P's recent sharp rise and potential pullback. For now, the author has decided to hold onto Fundsmith as a hedge but has placed it under review.

feed.title: Bitwise Files S-1 for Aptos ETF, CEO Cites ‘Momentum in Aptos Ecosystem’
feed.summary: Bitwise has filed an S-1 registration for an Aptos ETF, with CEO Hunter Horsley citing significant development momentum in the Aptos ecosystem. This follows initial administrative steps and comes as Aptos shows impressive performance metrics, including a surge in price and trading volume post-announcement, and strong transaction speeds that surpassed Solana and Sui Network. BlackRock's BUIDL fund has also expanded access to include Aptos, highlighting institutional interest.

feed.title: FEPI ETF (NYSEARCA:FEPI) Delivers 25.27% Yield and 15.3% YTD Gain as Tech Volatility Drives Record Income
feed.summary: The FEPI ETF (NYSEARCA:FEPI) offers a 25.27% annual yield with monthly dividends and has achieved a 15.3% year-to-date gain by leveraging tech volatility through a hybrid covered-call model. It strategically invests in volatile large-cap tech innovators like NVIDIA, Apple, and Tesla, generating substantial premium income through out-of-the-money call options. The ETF provides tax-deferred income through return of capital distributions and maintains NAV stability, positioning it as a strong performer in the high-yield ETF landscape for income-focused investors.

feed.title: Subdued Growth No Barrier To The Glimpse Group, Inc. (NASDAQ:VRAR) With Shares Advancing 26%
feed.summary: The Glimpse Group's stock (NASDAQ: VRAR) has seen a significant 26% increase in the past month, contributing to a 149% gain over the last year. Despite this, the company's price-to-sales (P/S) ratio of 3.5x is higher than the IT industry average, and its revenue growth and future forecasts are lower than industry peers. Investors are cautioned that the current elevated P/S ratio may not be sustainable given the subdued growth outlook.

feed.title: Amkor Technology Breaks Ground on New Semiconductor Advanced Packaging and Test Campus in Arizona; Expands Investment to $7 Billion
feed.summary: Amkor Technology, Inc. has broken ground on its new semiconductor advanced packaging and test campus in Arizona, expanding its planned investment to $7 billion across two phases. This significant project, supported by the Trump Administration's CHIPS for America Program, aims to strengthen U.S. semiconductor leadership and will create up to 3,000 jobs. The facility will be the first U.S.-based high-volume advanced packaging facility and will serve key customers like Apple and NVIDIA.

feed.title: Amkor breaks ground on $7 billion semiconductor test facility in Peoria, expected to create thousands of new jobs
feed.summary: Amkor Technology Inc. has broken ground on a $7 billion semiconductor test facility in Peoria, Arizona, which is expected to create 3,000 new jobs and become the first U.S.-based high-volume advanced packaging facility for the company. This investment is supported by the CHIPS for America Program and state and local governments, and it aims to strengthen America's advanced packaging capabilities for key customers like Apple and NVIDIA. The project is a collaborative effort between political parties, highlighting economic growth in the West Valley.

feed.title: Tesla App 4.49.6 Release Notes
feed.summary: The Tesla App 4.49.6 update, released October 7, 2025, introduces support for new "Standard Models" of the Model 3 and Y, dynamic vehicle models based on features, and hints at different front bumper cameras. The update also includes more signs of Unreal Engine integration for Autopilot visualizations, improved Dashcam streaming, and expanded in-app maintenance features. Furthermore, it brings updates for vehicle sharing, virtual queuing at Superchargers, and Robotaxi functionality, alongside iPhone 17 support, scheduling improvements, and changes to Tesla Insurance and Supercharger Mileage features.

feed.title: Gil Alexis II, Integral Ad Science, sells $35,904 in stock
feed.summary: Gil Alexis II, Chief Accounting Officer at Integral Ad Science (NASDAQ:IAS), sold 3,527 shares of common stock for $35,904 on October 3, 2025, according to a Form 4 filing. This transaction occurred as the company's stock has surged nearly 50% in the last six months, though InvestingPro analysis suggests it is trading in overbought territory. IAS recently reported strong Q2 2025 financial results and announced an agreement to be acquired by Novacap for $1.9 billion, prompting downgrades from Raymond James and Stifel due to valuation concerns.

feed.title: Tim Sweeney Declares End Of 'Scare Screens' As Google's Supreme Court Bid Over Android App Store Fails
feed.summary: The U.S. Supreme Court refused to block a lower court order requiring Google to open its Play Store to rival app marketplaces and allow external payment links, marking a significant win for Epic Games. Epic Games CEO Tim Sweeney hailed the decision, stating developers will soon be legally entitled to direct users to out-of-app payment options without fees and "scare screens." This mirrors a previous ruling against Apple, compelling it to allow alternative payment methods in its App Store.

feed.title: Walker Financial Buys $8 Million in Invesco NASDAQ 100 ETF as Tech Giants Keep Driving Markets
feed.summary: Walker Financial Services initiated a new position in the Invesco NASDAQ 100 ETF (QQQM) during the third quarter, purchasing 32,301 shares valued at approximately $8 million. This investment represents 3.1% of the firm's assets under management and highlights the continued institutional interest in large-cap growth stocks, particularly in the technology sector. The QQQM ETF, which tracks the NASDAQ-100 Index, has outperformed the S&P 500, driven by its top holdings such as Nvidia, Microsoft, Apple, and Amazon.

feed.title: AMD’s Stock Surges on Exciting News
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) stock surged by 10.22% due to positive market sentiment, driven by significant analyst upgrades and a breakthrough deal to supply OpenAI with 6 gigawatts of GPU power. This partnership, coupled with strong Q2 earnings and robust financial health indicators, positions AMD as a central player in AI growth. The article highlights analyst price target increases, with Barclays setting a $300 target and Roth Capital pushing to $250, reflecting confidence in AMD's strategic moves and multi-year processor supply agreements.

feed.title: Amkor Technology breaks ground on $7 billion semiconductor packaging facility near TSMC
feed.summary: Amkor Technology has broken ground on a new $7 billion semiconductor packaging and test facility in Peoria, Arizona, near the TSMC campus. This 750,000-square-foot plant is expected to create up to 3,000 jobs and will produce chips for companies like NVIDIA and Apple, primarily for artificial intelligence applications. The first phase of construction is anticipated to be completed in about two years, with chip production starting in 2028.

feed.title: AMD stock continues rally after OpenAI deal, now up 43% this week so far
feed.summary: AMD shares surged 11% on Wednesday, bringing its weekly gain to 43%, following an announcement that OpenAI plans to purchase billions of dollars worth of AI equipment from the chipmaker. This partnership has bolstered investor confidence that AMD can become a significant competitor to Nvidia in the AI chip market. The deal includes OpenAI potentially owning 10% of AMD, which AMD's CEO Lisa Su described as a "win-win."

feed.title: QUALCOMM Incorporated (QCOM) Sued Over Antitrust Violation on Smartphone Chips in the UK
feed.summary: QUALCOMM Incorporated is facing an antitrust lawsuit in the UK, accused of abusing its dominant market position in smartphone chipsets and patent licensing. A consumer group alleges Qualcomm charged inflated fees to manufacturers like Apple and Samsung, with these costs being passed on to consumers. The group is seeking compensation for affected consumers, estimated at £17 per phone.

feed.title: Is iShares MSCI ACWI Low Carbon Target ETF (CRBN) a Strong ETF Right Now?
feed.summary: The article evaluates the iShares MSCI ACWI Low Carbon Target ETF (CRBN) as a strong investment. It details CRBN's smart beta strategy, low expense ratio, significant assets under management, and strong year-to-date performance. The article also compares CRBN to alternative ESG ETFs and highlights its diversification and low-risk profile.

feed.title: Form FWP GOLDMAN SACHS GROUP INC Filed by: GOLDMAN SACHS GROUP INC
feed.summary: This FWP filing by Goldman Sachs Group Inc. details a Market Linked Securities offering, specifically Autocallable with Contingent Coupon and Contingent Downside Principal at Risk Securities linked to Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD) common stock, due October 19, 2028. The document outlines the summary of terms including pricing, payment structure, automatic call provisions, contingent coupon payments, and various risk factors associated with the investment, emphasizing that investors could lose a significant portion or all of their principal. It also specifies the roles of GS Finance Corp. as issuer and Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. as guarantor, and details the underwriting discount and tax considerations.

feed.title: Schwab U.S. Large-Cap Growth ETF (SCHG) Top 25 Holdings
feed.summary: The Schwab U.S. Large-Cap Growth ETF (SCHG) remains a popular choice for investors seeking exposure to the growth engine of the US economy, driven by major technology and innovation companies. Its top holdings include NVIDIA, Microsoft, and Apple, which collectively represent over 30% of the ETF's weight. SCHG has delivered a 15.3% year-to-date return in 2025, outperforming broader market ETFs due to strong gains in AI, cloud computing, and digital advertising, benefiting from expected interest rate cuts and a "soft landing" for the US economy.

feed.title: Ameren Corp (AEE) Declares $0.71 Quarterly Dividend; 2.7% Yield
feed.summary: Ameren Corp (AEE) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.71 per share, totaling an annualized dividend of $2.84. This dividend offers an annual yield of 2.7 percent, with payment scheduled for December 31, 2025, to shareholders of record on December 9, 2025. The ex-dividend date is set for December 8, 2025.

feed.title: A Cheap iPhone, the Clearwire Acquisition Drama, and the Battle Over Herbalife - AOL.com
feed.summary: This article discusses key business stories including Apple's potential release of a lower-cost iPhone and the ongoing acquisition drama between DISH Network and Sprint for Clearwire. It also highlights the battle between two hedge fund managers over Herbalife, predicting significant financial impact on one of the parties.

feed.title: TSMC Could Be Barred From Selling Chips To the U.S. Under China’s Newest ‘Rare Earth’ Export Control Measures, Disrupting the AI Industry - Wccftech
feed.summary: China's new rare earth export control measures could prevent TSMC from selling chips to US companies, potentially disrupting the AI industry. These restrictions, effective November 8, would require export licenses for chip manufacturers reliant on Chinese rare earth materials, impacting global giants like TSMC, SK Hynix, and Samsung and affecting the supply chain for companies like NVIDIA, AMD, and Apple. Taiwan's economy ministry has clarified its position on these developments.

feed.title: Where Is MP Materials Headed After Stunning 378% Rally and Apple Partnership News? - Yahoo Finance
feed.summary: MP Materials has seen a remarkable 378% rally over the past year, driven by the increasing importance of rare earth materials for clean tech and defense. Despite this growth, traditional valuation methods like Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) and Price-to-Book (P/B) ratios suggest the stock is significantly overvalued. Simply Wall St introduces "Narratives" as a better way for investors to assess the company's worth by customizing future projections.

feed.title: Key facts: Apple Inc. ranks in top 10 S&P 500 stocks; holds AAA rating; Buyback Aristocrat
feed.summary: Apple Inc. (AAPL) is a prominent S&P 500 stock, holding a significant share in the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust and an AAA credit rating from Moody's and S&P. The company generates substantial free cash flow and is recognized as a Buyback Aristocrat due to its consistent reduction in share count, signaling a strong commitment to shareholder returns.

feed.title: AI boom puts Taiwan's semiconductor sector at centre of world's biggest flashpoint - This is Money
feed.summary: Taiwan's crucial role in global semiconductor production, especially advanced chips for AI, places it at the center of a geopolitical flashpoint with China. An invasion or blockade by China could trigger a global economic crisis, severely impacting the AI revolution and stock markets. Taiwanese officials emphasize the difficulty of relocating or replacing their chip industry, which they consider integral to national security and identity.

feed.title: Financial Services Company Wealth Oklahoma Began Investing in Allison Transmission. Is the Stock a Buy? - AOL.com
feed.summary: Wealth Oklahoma, formerly Stolper Co, initiated a new position in Allison Transmission Holdings (NYSE:ALSN) during Q3 2025, purchasing 75,606 shares valued at an estimated $6.4 million. This investment, representing nearly 2% of Wealth Oklahoma's 13F reportable assets, comes as Allison Transmission's stock has underperformed the S&P 500 and is near a 52-week low. The move suggests Wealth Oklahoma sees potential for a rebound in the propulsion systems technology company, despite a recent cut in its 2025 revenue outlook.

feed.title: Apple Music, Spotify or YouTube Music? Which offers the best value? - ABS-CBN
feed.summary: This article compares Apple Music, Spotify, and YouTube Music to determine which offers the best value for Filipinos, considering subscription pricing, unique features, content availability, and audio quality. It details the plans and advantages of each platform, highlighting Spotify's popularity with an ad-supported free tier, Apple Music's affordability and superior audio quality, and YouTube Music's integration with the broader YouTube platform. The comparison aims to help users decide based on their budget and preferences.

feed.title: Warren Buffett Has $193 Billion Invested in 9 Forever Stocks. Here's the Best of the Bunch. - AOL.com
feed.summary: Warren Buffett has invested approximately $138 billion in nine "forever stocks," which he considers outstanding businesses with outstanding managements. These stocks include long-held positions like American Express and Coca-Cola, as well as more recent additions like the Japanese trading houses and Apple. While Marubeni scores well across multiple metrics, the author argues that Apple stands out as the best of the bunch due to its long-term growth potential in new product categories and AI.

feed.title: Jamf’s (JAMF) Buy Rating Reiterated at Needham & Company LLC
feed.summary: Needham & Company LLC reiterated a "buy" rating on Jamf (JAMF) stock in a recent report. JPMorgan Chase & Co. also previously issued an "overweight" rating but cut its price target to $12.00 from $15.00. Institutional investors have been actively trading JAMF shares, with several increasing their stakes.

feed.title: Science committee summons Korea telecom CEOs for hacking, security audit - CHOSUNBIZ - Chosun Biz
feed.summary: South Korea's Science, ICT, Broadcasting, and Communications Committee will summon the CEOs of the three major domestic mobile carriers (SK Telecom, KT, LG Uplus) for a National Assembly audit. The audit, commencing on the 13th, will primarily focus on recent large-scale hacking incidents and security vulnerabilities experienced by these companies. Additionally, the committee will investigate the fairness of KT's president appointment process and address issues with global big tech companies like Google and Apple.

feed.title: Why Axcelis Technologies Stock Plummeted Today
feed.summary: Axcelis Technologies' stock fell sharply despite beating Q4 analyst estimates, largely due to a disappointing Q1 forward guidance and a cautious industry outlook. The company anticipates reduced demand in the short term, particularly from existing customer supplies and soft demand in China, though it expects moderate improvement in the latter half of the year driven by silicon carbide and memory recovery.

feed.title: Old Port Advisors Has $7.17 Million Position in Apple Inc. $AAPL
feed.summary: Old Port Advisors has trimmed its stake in Apple Inc., reflecting broader institutional investor activity in the company. Other major investors like Vanguard Group Inc., Kingstone Capital Partners Texas LLC, and Northern Trust Corp have significantly adjusted their holdings. The article also covers insider stock sales, Apple's recent dividend announcement, price performance, and updated analyst ratings for the company.

feed.title: M5 iPad Pro Was Listed On AT&T’s Website Briefly, Signaling A Launch That May Transpire Alongside The Updated MacBook Soon
feed.summary: An AT&T listing for the M5 iPad Pro briefly appeared and was then removed, suggesting an imminent launch for Apple's updated tablet. This follows an earlier unboxing video of the device, with speculation that AT&T's listing might have been an SEO tactic. The M5 iPad Pro is expected to show significant GPU performance gains over its M4 predecessor.

feed.title: 19-Year-Old Raises 120 Million Yuan in Financing - 36Kr
feed.summary: Serena Ge, a 19-year-old co-founder and CEO of AI data company Datacurve, has raised $17.7 million (approximately 126 million RMB) in financing within one year of the company's establishment. Datacurve focuses on providing high-quality code data to support large AI models, using a unique "bounty hunter" system to attract skilled software engineers. The article highlights a growing trend of post-2000s entrepreneurs dominating the AI world, securing significant funding and pushing the boundaries of AI capabilities.

feed.title: Want $1 Million in Retirement? 2 Simple Index Funds to Buy and Hold for Decades. - AOL.com

feed.title: Where Will Apple (AAPL) Be in 5 Years? - AOL.com
feed.summary: This article analyzes the future prospects of Apple (AAPL) over the next five years, suggesting that its stock may underperform the broader market despite its strong brand and ecosystem. The author highlights concerns about slowing sales growth and a high valuation (P/E ratio of 39.2) as key headwinds. While acknowledging Apple's past success and status as Berkshire Hathaway's largest position, the piece advises investors to temper expectations for market-beating returns in the coming half-decade.

feed.title: Prediction: 2 Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks That Will Be Worth More Than Palantir by the End of 2026
feed.summary: This article predicts that two AI stocks, Alibaba Group (BABA) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), will surpass Palantir Technologies (PLTR) in market capitalization by the end of 2026. The author argues that Palantir's current valuation of $441 billion is not supported by its fundamentals, while Alibaba and AMD show strong AI-driven growth and more reasonable valuations, despite AMD currently having a lower market cap. Alibaba's AI-related product sales are seeing triple-digit growth, and AMD is becoming a significant player in the AI chip market due to its partnership with OpenAI.

The concern stems from the index's heavy concentration in a few large-cap tech companies, reducing its diversity and increasing risk during sector downturns. Financial advisors now recommend broader diversification beyond just the S&P 500 to protect investments, especially for those nearing retirement.

Analysts, however, view this as a policy shock rather than an underlying financial crisis, suggesting potential buying opportunities amidst the panic.

feed.title: How to move the search bar to the top on iOS 26 - Mashable
feed.summary: Apple's new iOS 26 update has sparked user complaints regarding its Liquid Glass design and the relocation of the device's search bar to the bottom of the screen. While many aspects of iOS 26 are customizable, the general search bar location is not. However, users can still adjust the Safari search bar's position in settings to return it to the traditional top placement.
