feed.title: Microsoft 365 Premium Marks the Next Phase of AI Monetization
feed.summary: Microsoft is expanding the availability of its AI assistant, Copilot, to individual users of Microsoft 365 Premium for $19.99 per month, marking a new phase in AI monetization. This strategy mirrors their successful rollout of the Microsoft 365 suite, aiming to convert AI functionality into recurring revenue. Analysts predict Microsoft's AI products could generate over $10 billion in annual revenue by 2026, leveraging the company's strong position in productivity software and enterprise penetration despite potential challenges like tightening IT budgets and regulatory scrutiny.

feed.title: Apple Nears Acquisition of UC Berkeley–Backed AI Startup Prompt AI
feed.summary: Apple is reportedly close to acquiring Prompt AI, a UC Berkeley-backed startup specializing in computer vision and home security software. This acquisition is seen as an acquihire to enhance Apple's HomeKit ecosystem and smart home features, particularly in light of Apple's plans for its own home security camera. Prompt AI's decision to shut down its Seemour app and delete user data highlights Apple's continued emphasis on privacy and on-device processing for its integrated technologies.

feed.title: Apple’s Second-Generation Smart Glasses Could Run Two Operating Systems, But That Will Depend On Which Device It Is Tethered To - Wccftech
feed.summary: Apple's second-generation smart glasses are rumored to be highly versatile, capable of running different operating systems depending on whether they are tethered to a Mac or an iPhone. While the first generation might lack a display, the successor aims to offer mixed-reality content with a full visionOS experience when connected to a Mac and a lighter platform with an iPhone. The company is exploring both wired and wireless tethering solutions, acknowledging the potential challenges of a non-standalone device.

feed.title: Analyst Explains ‘The Problem’ With Apple (AAPL) - Insider Monkey

Macquarie Core Equity Fund also noted that Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL) underperformed the S&P 500 recently due to maturing products and slower growth rates. The article highlights that while Apple faces challenges, particularly in the AI race, other AI stocks may offer higher and faster returns.

feed.title: Analyst Explains ‘The Problem’ With Apple (AAPL) - Insider Monkey
feed.summary: An analyst discusses the challenges facing Apple Inc. (AAPL), noting that its recent innovations, such as the iPhone 17, are incremental and might not excite investors sufficiently to drive a "super cycle," especially compared to advancements in AI by competitors like Nvidia and Google. This deal was reported on October 13, 2025. The article highlights significant savings for consumers looking to purchase the popular Apple earbuds.

feed.title: Say Goodbye to Apple TV+ as Major Streaming Service Officially Announces 'Vibrant New Identity'
feed.summary: Apple TV+ is rebranding to "Apple TV" as announced in an _F1_ press release, marking a significant identity change for the streaming service. This rebranding follows a recent price hike and comes amid discussions of potential new features like an ad-supported tier. The change also coincides with other major streaming shake-ups, such as Disney's plans to merge Hulu into Disney+ by 2026.

feed.title: ARM Stock Surge: Buy or Wait? - StocksToTrade
feed.summary: ARM Holdings plc (NASDAQ: ARM) stocks have surged by 11.05% due to optimism around AI chip advancements, with its Q2 fiscal year 2026 financial results set for November 5, 2025. Despite a lowered price target of $171 by Morgan Stanley, an Overweight rating is maintained, recognizing ARM's licensing and custom chip potential, while SoftBank's $5B margin loan using ARM shares highlights strategic AI ambitions. The article discusses key market developments, financial snapshots, and market forces shaping ARM’s trajectory, emphasizing strong licensing revenue, Apple's iPhone demand, and Qualcomm's adoption of ARM's architecture.

feed.title: Nilsine Partners Boosts Stake in QQXT as Investors Look Beyond Big Tech - The Motley Fool
feed.summary: Nilsine Partners, LLC increased its stake in the First Trust NASDAQ-100 Ex-Technology Sector Index Fund (QQXT) by 61,385 shares, a trade worth an estimated $6.07 million. This move signals the firm's continued confidence in large-cap U.S. stocks beyond the technology sector, as QQXT offers diversified exposure to non-tech constituents of the NASDAQ-100. The fund provides a balanced investment strategy, reducing reliance on mega-cap tech companies and potentially enhancing portfolio resilience when market leadership shifts.

feed.title: Google Wallet picks up support for American Airlines boarding passes - Android Authority
feed.summary: American Airlines now supports adding boarding passes directly to Google Wallet, a feature previously unavailable. This integration allows for automatic updates to flight details, a significant convenience for travelers. This move catches American Airlines up with other major players in offering Google Wallet support for boarding passes.

feed.title: US Equity Indexes Rebound After Bessent Breathes Life Into Trump-Xi Trade Talks in South Korea - MarketScreener
feed.summary: US equity indexes rebounded following reports that Bessent breathed life into Trump-Xi trade talks in South Korea. This news led to a positive shift in market sentiment, with major indexes like the S&P 500, Dow Jones, and NASDAQ 100 all showing significant gains. The rebound comes after earlier concerns regarding US-China trade tensions, indicating that any perceived de-escalation can strongly influence stock performance.

feed.title: Apple TV+ Rebrand To “Apple TV” And Vibrant New Icon Hint At Upcoming Hardware And A Unified Ecosystem - Wccftech
feed.summary: Apple has rebranded its streaming service from "Apple TV+" to "Apple TV" and introduced new, more colorful icons. This move, along with hints at new M5-powered devices and an updated Apple TV hardware, suggests Apple is aiming for a tightly integrated, unified entertainment ecosystem. The change simplifies branding and prepares for a major push into the living room, potentially linking traditional streaming with spatial computing via the Vision Pro.

feed.title: Why Datadog (DDOG) Stock Is Up Today - TradingView
feed.summary: Datadog (DDOG) shares rose by 2.9% after the company announced expanded support and new integrations for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI), specifically enhancing AI and machine learning workload management. These new features include GPU Monitoring, Cloud Cost Management, and Cloud SIEM, positioning Datadog as a key observability vendor for OCI's AI infrastructure. This positive development follows a contract renewal with OpenAI and increased price targets from analysts, reinforcing investor confidence in the company's growth in cloud consumption and service usage.

feed.title: Nilsine Partners Boosts Stake in QQXT as Investors Look Beyond Big Tech - Mitrade
feed.summary: Nilsine Partners, LLC increased its stake in the First Trust NASDAQ-100 Ex-Technology Sector Index Fund (QQXT) by purchasing 61,385 shares, valued at approximately $6.07 million. This move signals confidence in large-cap U.S. stocks outside the technology sector and diversifies the firm's portfolio by giving equal weight to sectors like consumer goods, healthcare, and industrials. The ETF, QQXT, tracks the NASDAQ-100 Ex-Technology Sector Index, offering investors exposure to U.S. equities without heavy reliance on big tech.

feed.title: Premium And AI Phones Powered Global Smartphone Sales In 2025
feed.summary: Global smartphone shipments increased by 2.6% in Q3 2025, driven by strong demand for premium and AI-powered devices from companies like Apple and Samsung. Despite economic uncertainties, consumers are investing in high-end smartphones, indicating market resilience and sustained growth for the tech sector. This trend highlights how innovation and strategic promotions can overcome broader economic challenges.

feed.title: Apple In Talks To Buy Prompt AI Talent
feed.summary: Apple is reportedly in advanced talks to acquihire Prompt AI's team and technology. The 11-employee startup, which previously raised $5 million, had also received bids from xAI and Neuralink. This potential acquisition fits Apple's strategy of acquiring talent and tools without large balance sheet impacts, potentially speeding up on-device AI features.

feed.title: Jefferies Flags Margin Risk For Apple iPhone 17
feed.summary: Jefferies analysts highlight potential margin risks for Apple's iPhone 17 due to changing smartphone tariff exemptions and the possibility of increased import duties from China and Vietnam. The report also notes cooling momentum for high-end iPhone 17 Pro models and a shift towards a base-heavy mix, which could compress Average Selling Prices (ASPs) as tariff risks intensify. Apple's significant U.S. investment may not guarantee exemptions and could lead to pressure for more domestic iPhone assembly, further impacting margins.

feed.title: Apple Inc. (AAPL) Stock: Climbs as China Preorders for iPhone Air Set to Begin October 17 - parameter.io
feed.summary: Apple Inc. (AAPL) stock climbed as the company prepares to launch the eSIM-only iPhone Air in China. Preorders are set to begin on October 17, with the official retail launch on October 22, after Chinese telecom providers received approval for trial eSIM services. This move marks Apple's strategic push for eSIM adoption in the significant Chinese market, despite initial delays.

feed.title: American Airlines adding Google Wallet support for boarding passes - 9to5Google
feed.summary: American Airlines has announced that it will support Google Wallet for its boarding passes, allowing customers to add their passes and receive flight updates. This is an overdue addition that enhances convenience for travelers, building on Google Wallet's existing capability to add boarding passes via screenshots. The airline is also improving Apple Wallet support and "reimagining the customer experience" with new AI tools and 3D plane maps.

feed.title: Volvo EX30 Owners Rage Over iPhone-Only Digital Key System
feed.summary: Owners of the new Volvo EX30 electric vehicle are expressing outrage over its digital key system, which reportedly works exclusively with iPhones, leaving Android users unable to unlock or start their cars. This iPhone-centric approach, stemming from Volvo's collaboration with Apple, has led to calls for broader compatibility and even contemplation by Volvo execs to abandon the digital key experiment altogether amidst the backlash. The controversy highlights the challenges of integrating smartphone features into the automotive industry and the need for cross-platform accessibility to maintain customer satisfaction.

feed.title: The Goldman Sachs (GS) Stock: Surges as $7B Industry Ventures Acquisition and Q3 Earnings Optimism Fuel Growth
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs (GS) stock surged 2.93% following the announcement of its acquisition of Industry Ventures, a venture capital firm managing $7 billion in assets. The deal, valued at up to $965 million, is expected to enhance Goldman's presence in venture capital and alternative investments, particularly in early-stage and growth-focused tech. This strategic move, coupled with optimism for upcoming Q3 earnings, fueled the stock's rise and strengthened confidence in the firm's strategic direction.

feed.title: Technology Stocks To Watch Now - October 10th - MarketBeat

The rebrand is seen as Apple catching up to how people already refer to their streaming content.

This positive data reinforces optimism for Apple's future performance, particularly looking ahead to fiscal year 2026, despite expectations of a slight miss on fourth-quarter iPhone estimates. Munster anticipates that first-quarter numbers will more than compensate for any near-term shortfalls, driven by continued high demand.

This move aims to increase user retention and engagement by offering interactive experiences like party games, thereby expanding its cultural footprint beyond traditional streaming. The company sees this as a way to convert passive viewers into active participants, ensuring it remains relevant in a saturated streaming market without increasing subscription costs.

Macrohard will focus on defining operating systems and product requirements, leveraging third-party manufacturers, similar to Apple's model. xAI plans to develop an AI-centric operating system and aims to release an AI-generated game by the end of next year, supported by its Colossus infrastructure.

feed.title: Apple expands renewable energy projects across Europe - Apple
feed.summary: Apple is expanding its clean energy projects across Europe with new solar and wind farms in Greece, Italy, Latvia, Poland, and Romania, adding 650 MW of renewable energy capacity. These projects, costing over $600 million, aim to generate over 1 million MWh of clean electricity by 2030 to match customer electricity use for Apple products, contributing to Apple's 2030 carbon-neutral goal. Lisa Jackson, Apple's VP of Environment, Policy, and Social Initiatives, highlighted the goal to power Apple devices with 100% clean electricity by 2030, enhancing energy security and supporting local economies.

feed.title: New EU solar initiative doesn't scratch the surface of what Apple is consuming - AppleInsider
feed.summary: Apple has announced new solar and wind energy projects in several European countries, aiming to match the energy consumption of iPhones and Macs by 2030. While these initiatives contribute to Apple's carbon-neutral goals, the article argues they do not adequately address the much larger energy demands of its data centers, especially with the rise of AI. The company has also faced scrutiny and removed carbon neutrality claims for some products after accusations of "greenwashing."

feed.title: Is Quality about to make a comeback? - Barclays Private Bank
feed.summary: This article and podcast discuss why "Quality stocks," characterized by consistent earnings, high profitability, and stable balance sheets, may be poised for a comeback after lagging behind mega-cap AI stocks. Experts from Barclays Private Bank argue that the recent underperformance of quality stocks is cyclical, not structural, and represents a buying opportunity given current market concentration and valuation premiums. The discussion highlights catalysts such as narrowing earnings growth gaps, macro uncertainty, and changing market dynamics that could drive this rotation.

feed.title: IDC: 3Q Global Smartphone Shipments Grow by 2.6%; Apple Shipments Hit Historical New High - AASTOCKS.com
feed.summary: Global smartphone shipments rose by 2.6% to 322.7 million units in Q3 2025, primarily due to strong demand for high-end and AI-supported devices. Apple's shipments reached a historical new high during this period. IDC anticipates continued strong sales for the rest of the year, fueled by promotional activities, AI enhancements, and ongoing device innovation.

feed.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook tours China as iPhone Air sales begin - The Star

This release coincides with CEO Tim Cook's visit to China, where he engaged with local figures and made a donation to Tsinghua University. The delayed launch in China compared to other markets is due to local carriers' slower adoption of eSIM technology.

feed.title: Apple announces major expansion of renewables investment across Europe
feed.summary: Apple is significantly expanding its renewable energy investments across Europe, funding large-scale solar and wind farms in countries like Greece, Italy, and Poland. This initiative is part of Apple's ambitious goal to match 100% of the electricity used by its European customers' devices with clean energy by 2030 and achieve carbon neutrality across its entire value chain. The firm estimates these new projects will generate over 1,000 GWh annually, funding which has now reached $600 million (£450 million).

feed.title: Apple Sued Over Use of Copyrighted Books to Train Apple Intelligence - Insurance Journal
feed.summary: Apple is facing a lawsuit from two neuroscientists who claim the company misused thousands of copyrighted books, including their own, to train its Apple Intelligence AI model. The lawsuit, filed in California federal court, alleges Apple used "shadow libraries" of pirated books for AI training. This adds to a growing list of copyright infringement cases against tech companies over AI training data.

feed.title: Apple Pivots to AI Smart Glasses Launch by 2027, Ditching Vision Pro Updates - WebProNews
feed.summary: Apple is shifting its focus from updating the Vision Pro to developing AI-driven smart glasses, potentially launching them by 2026-2027 to compete with Meta's Ray-Ban. These lightweight devices are expected to integrate with iPhones, offering AR features like object recognition and voice controls. This strategic move aims to boost consumer adoption and demand amidst slowing iPhone sales and the high price point of the Vision Pro.

feed.title: Microsoft Enhances Windows 11 AI for Copilot+ PCs with On-Device NPUs - WebProNews
feed.summary: Microsoft is significantly improving AI integration in Windows 11 for Copilot+ PCs, focusing on an enhanced AI agent in the Settings app that leverages on-device NPUs for contextual suggestions and automation. This advancement positions Windows as an AI-native platform, offering privacy-focused computing, but also creates accessibility challenges due to its exclusivity to NPU-equipped hardware. The move aims to streamline user experience and potentially influence future OS designs, despite criticisms regarding hardware dependency.

feed.title: Apple expands renewable energy projects across Europe - Apple
feed.summary: Apple is significantly expanding its clean energy projects across Europe with new large-scale solar and wind farms in Greece, Italy, Latvia, Poland, and Romania, adding 650 megawatts of renewable energy capacity. These projects, along with a new operational solar array in Spain, will generate over 1 million megawatt-hours of clean electricity for Apple users by 2030, contributing to Apple's 2030 goal of being carbon neutral across its entire footprint. The initiative aims to match 100 percent of global customer electricity use with clean electricity, addressing the 29 percent of Apple's greenhouse gas emissions attributed to product use in 2024.

This update signals an imminent public launch and aims to unify experiences across Apple's ecosystem, particularly by bridging the gap between iPad and traditional computing. The beta phase is crucial for testing and feedback, with expectations for potential hardware synergies and continuous improvements leading to a more integrated software future.

feed.title: Apple expands renewable energy projects across Europe - Apple

feed.title: Move Over, Gemini Nano Banana, Microsoft’s New AI Image Generator Is Here To Compete - Mashable India
feed.summary: Microsoft has launched MAI-Image-1, its first in-house text-to-image generator, designed to compete with Google's Gemini Nano Banana and OpenAI's tools. This new AI focuses on producing faster, more photorealistic visuals, particularly for elements like lightning and landscapes, and aims to overcome repetitive AI-generated styles. feed.title: Global big tech companies have been summoned to the parliamentary audit due to allegations of tax av.. - 매일경제

Lawmakers are pushing for systematic changes, including the enactment of a digital service tax law and stricter enforcement mechanisms for tax audits, to address the outflow of national wealth.

feed.title: Apple hit with new copyright lawsuit over material used to train its AI

This marks the second such lawsuit against Apple in less than a month concerning the training of its Apple Intelligence models.

feed.title: Apple hits highest ever third quarter as global smartphone market grows 3%

feed.title: AMD and Intel teaming up to stick it to Arm has let to 'significant progress' improving x86 after just a year - PC Gamer
feed.summary: AMD and Intel formed the x86 Ecosystem Advisory Group (EAG) one year ago to improve the x86 architecture and counter Arm's growing influence. This collaboration has led to significant progress, specifically with the development of AVX10 for improved vector performance, ACE for standardized matrix multiplication for AI workloads, and ChkTag for enhanced memory safety, bringing x86 closer to Arm's capabilities. These advancements aim to provide customers with more reasons to invest in Intel and AMD products in the competitive chip market.

feed.title: SEPTEMBER SPEND TRENDS | UK Unlocked - Barclays Group

However, some retail categories like furniture, pharmacy, health & beauty, and clothing showed resilience, while consumers also prepared for the Golden Quarter and November's Autumn Budget.

feed.title: Apple sued over use of copyrighted books to train Apple Intelligence - Arizona Digital Free Press

This initiative is part of Apple's 2030 goal to be carbon neutral across its entire footprint and match 100 percent of global customer electricity use with clean electricity. The projects will generate over 1 million megawatt-hours of clean electricity on behalf of Apple users by 2030 and involve substantial financial investment.

This acquisition increases the bank's total stake in Eli Lilly to 14,521 shares, worth $11.08 million, and now accounts for 1.42% of its reportable U.S. equity AUM. The bank has been consistently adding to its Eli Lilly position for at least nine consecutive quarters, seeing it as a key player in the obesity and diabetes markets.

feed.title: Dynamic Advisor Solutions Dumps 26K QQQ Shares Worth $15 Million - The Motley Fool

This transaction reduced their QQQ holdings to 71,443 shares, now worth $42.89 million, and represents a rebalancing of their portfolio, rather than a negative outlook on QQQ itself, given the ETF's strong performance. QQQ remains one of the company's top holdings, complementing riskier tech stocks in their diverse ETF-heavy portfolio.

feed.title: Dynamic Advisor Solutions Dumps 26K QQQ Shares Worth $15 Million - The Globe and Mail
feed.summary: Dynamic Advisor Solutions LLC sold 26,223 shares of Invesco QQQ Trust (NASDAQ:QQQ) during Q3 2025, valued at approximately $15.02 million. feed.title: Barnes County man accused of using AI to generate child sex abuse materials set to change his plea - Valley News Live
feed.summary: A Barnes County man, Mitchell Kohler, charged with five counts of possession of prohibited materials, is scheduled to change his plea after initially pleading not guilty. Kohler was arrested when Apple Inc. reported suspected child sex abuse material, and he later admitted to using AI to create images and videos of teenage girls from his community using their Instagram photos. He is set to change his plea on October 20, with a felony jury trial tentatively scheduled for October 21.

This initiative, which has already paid out $35 million to over 800 researchers, is part of Apple's strategy to stay ahead of cyber threats by engaging a global network of security experts.

feed.title: Apple lobbies India to change tax law seen hindering its expansion, sources say - MarketScreener
feed.summary: Apple is lobbying the Indian government to amend its income tax law to avoid taxation on high-end iPhone machinery it provides to contract manufacturers, a move crucial for its expansion in India. The current law could subject Apple to significant Indian taxes on its iPhone profits if it owns equipment within the country, unlike the arrangement it has in China. India is cautiously reviewing the request, balancing the need for foreign investment with safeguarding its sovereign right to tax.

feed.title: Exclusive: Apple lobbies India to change tax law seen hindering its expansion, sources say - beritasriwijaya.co.id

The company aims to create a more favorable regulatory environment to streamline operations, increase market penetration, and boost revenue growth in India. This strategic move highlights Apple's commitment to navigating complex global regulatory frameworks for sustained growth.

feed.title: Exclusive-Apple lobbies India to change tax law seen hindering its expansion, sources say - Yahoo Finance
feed.summary: Apple is lobbying the Indian government to amend its income tax law to avoid being taxed on high-end iPhone machinery it provides to contract manufacturers. This change is crucial for Apple's future expansion in India, as the current law could classify Apple's ownership of equipment as a "business connection," making its iPhone profits liable for Indian taxes. The issue is seen as a significant hurdle as Apple aims to diversify its manufacturing beyond China and increase its presence in India, the world's second-largest mobile market.

feed.title: Microsoft, Tear Down That Wall! - Cory Doctorow – Medium

feed.title: Pricing Stays Private: Appellate Court Draws the Line on FRAND Transparency - Lewis Silkin
feed.summary: The Court of Appeal has issued important guidance on the redaction of confidential information in FRAND (Fair, Reasonable, and Non-Discriminatory) judgments, especially regarding third-party material. The court ruled that financial terms such as lump-sum payments and unpacked per-unit rates from third-party licenses must be redacted to prevent commercial harm, affirming that open justice admits limited exceptions for confidential information. This decision emphasizes a pragmatic approach in FRAND cases, prioritizing the protection of granular commercial terms while still requiring justification for redaction through a case-specific balancing exercise.

feed.title: Can Apple Make Success Of Vision Pro Yet? Ming-Chi Kuo Calls M5 Chip The 'Most Important' Hardware Upgrade - Benzinga
feed.summary: Analyst Ming-Chi Kuo reaffirmed his forecast that Apple's M5 chip is the most crucial hardware upgrade for the Vision Pro, despite the device's ongoing struggles to gain market traction. Apple recently unveiled the M5 chip, powering updated versions of the MacBook Pro, iPad Pro, and the Vision Pro headset, all aimed at enhancing AI and graphics performance. However, Vision Pro sales have been underwhelming, leading Apple to reportedly pause development on a cheaper model to focus on smart glasses.

feed.title: Zacks Research Forecasts Apple’s Q3 Earnings (NASDAQ:AAPL) - Defense World
feed.summary: Zacks Research anticipates Apple's Q3 earnings per share to be $1.76, while the company maintains a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating among analysts with a target price of $248.29. The article details recent analyst ratings, AAPL's stock performance, a recent dividend announcement, and a significant insider stock sale by SVP Deirdre O’brien.

feed.title: Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) Surpasses Q3 Earnings Expectations with Strong Growth
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSM) reported outstanding third-quarter results, exceeding earnings and revenue expectations with a 40.8% year-over-year revenue increase to $33.1 billion. The company demonstrated strong financial health with high gross and operating margins, driven significantly by advanced technology nodes contributing 74% of wafer revenue. A NT$5.00 dividend was approved, reflecting robust financial performance and shareholder value.

feed.title: Can Apple Make Success Of Vision Pro Yet? Ming-Chi Kuo Calls M5 Chip The 'Most Important' Hardware Upgrade - inkl
feed.summary: Analyst Ming-Chi Kuo believes the M5 chip is the most crucial hardware upgrade for Apple's Vision Pro, despite the device's ongoing struggles to gain market traction. Apple recently launched the M5 chip, powering new MacBook Pro, iPad Pro, and an upgraded Vision Pro, with enhanced AI and graphics capabilities. The updated Vision Pro faces challenges from underwhelming sales, though Apple CEO Tim Cook remains committed to the product.

feed.title: Apple  launches iPad Pro, Vision Pro and MacBook Pro with M5 Chip
feed.summary: Apple has updated its iPad Pro, Vision Pro, and entry-level MacBook Pro devices with the new M5 chip, enhancing computing speeds, graphics, and AI capabilities. These refreshed products are available for preorder and will be in stores on October 22, just in time for the holiday season. The company also announced that the iPhone Air will launch in China on the same day.

feed.title: AI boom delivers record net profit for Taiwan's TSMC - The Sanford Herald
authors: with Katie Forster in Tokyo AFP
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) reported a record net profit for the third quarter, driven by surging demand for microchips used in AI and iPhones. The company's net profit reached NT$452.3 billion (US$14.7 billion), exceeding analyst expectations. This growth comes amidst escalating trade tensions between Washington and Beijing concerning chip technology and export restrictions.

feed.title: AI boom delivers record net profit for Taiwan's TSMC - High Point Enterprise
authors: with Katie Forster in Tokyo AFP
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) reported a record net profit for the third quarter due to surging demand for microchips driven by the AI boom and iPhone production. The company's net profit soared to $16.7 billion, exceeding analyst expectations, with third-quarter revenue also surpassing forecasts. The increasing investment in AI, particularly from clients like Nvidia and Apple, is a significant growth driver despite trade tensions and export restrictions impacting the Chinese market.

feed.title: Delta Capital Management Bets Big on Deere & Company (NYSE: DE) in Q3

feed.title: Taiwanese chipmaker TSMC sees nearly 40% jump in its net profit thanks to the AI boom
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Corp. (TSMC) reported a nearly 40% jump in net profit for the last quarter, reaching a record $15 billion, driven by surging demand for AI-related semiconductors. Despite geopolitical tensions, including China-U.S. trade friction and U.S. Commerce Secretary proposals for chip production splits, TSMC's dominance and ongoing investments in the U.S. and Japan are expected to maintain its strong market position. The company remains a key supplier to tech giants like Apple and Nvidia.

feed.title: Meta Poaches Apple's Siri Strategist, Ke Yang, as Talent War Explodes - MobileAppDaily
feed.summary: Meta has reportedly poached Ke Yang, a key strategist behind Apple's Siri, signaling a significant escalation in the AI talent war. Yang, who led Apple's critical AI models team, joins a growing list of Apple AI specialists moving to Meta, including Ruoming Pang, the founder of Apple's Foundation Models team. This brain drain poses a challenge for Apple as it races to integrate generative AI and improve Siri amidst fierce competition from Meta, Google, and OpenAI.
